sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual for H&abyz G1417/G1717 Series wireless digital flat panel detectors, including intended use, product specifications, operation, maintenance, EMC, labeling, warranty, and revision history.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports setup, operation, safe use, maintenance, and regulatory/EMC information for G1417/G1717 Series detector models.
keyPoints: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors used for screening and diagnosis of disease or injury by qualified/trained doctors or technicians.
The detectors are not intended for mammography applications.
The manual lists detector model variants G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, and G1717FWP.
The equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.
Revision history states Version 0.0 dated 2024-07-22 as the initial release.

concepts.evidence: When disposing of this product, therefore, be absolutely sure to follow the procedure that conforms to the laws and regulations applicable in your area.

evidence.claim: The document is a user manual for G1417/G1717 Series detector models made by H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: This user manual explains how to use the G1417/G1717 Series detector models made by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The manual is intended for users who set up and operate the G1417/G1717 Series detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: This manual is intended for the users who set up and operate the G1417/G1717 Series detector models.

evidence.claim: G1417/G1717 Series detectors are digital flat panel detectors for screening and diagnosis of disease or injury.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors that is used for screening and diagnosis of disease of injury.

evidence.claim: The detectors are intended for use by qualified or trained doctors or technicians on adult subjects for diagnostic radiographic exposures of multiple body parts.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are intended for use by a qualified/trained doctor or technician on both adult subjects for taking diagnostic radiographic exposures of the skull, spinal column, chest, abdomen, extremities, and other body parts.

evidence.claim: G1417/G1717 Series is used for patients with or suspected of muscle and bone injury or respiratory diseases.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417/G1717 Series is used to examine in patients with or suspected of muscle and bone injury, respiratory diseases.

evidence.claim: The detectors are not intended for mammography applications.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.

evidence.claim: The product is identified as a medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a Medical Device.

evidence.claim: G1417/G1717 Series detectors support IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac wireless communication.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417/G1717 Series detectors support IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac to acquire images without a wired connection in anytime, anywhere.

evidence.claim: The protection against electrical shock is Class I or internally powered ME equipment, with Class B applied part degree of protection.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of protection against electrical shock: Class l or Internal powered ME equipment Degree of protection against electrical shock: Class B applied part

evidence.claim: Only a physician or legally certified operator should use the product.
evidence.evidenceText: Only a physician or a legally certified operator should use this product.

evidence.claim: The detector product family includes G1417 and G1717 wireless detector models.
The listed detector model variants include G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model Name G1417CW G1417CWP G1417FW G1417FWP G1717CW G1717CWP G1717FW G1717FWP

evidence.claim: The battery pack model is MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ with 3330mAh capacity.
evidence.evidenceText: Model: MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ Capacity: 3330mAh

evidence.claim: The detector IP rating is IP68.
evidence.evidenceText: IP Range IP68

evidence.claim: The operating environment is 10~35℃, 20~75% H.R., and 700~1060hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation Temperature 10~35℃ Humidity 20~75% H.R. Atmospheric pressure 700~1060hPa

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime may be up to five years with appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime may be up to five (5) years under the appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Regular inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: The equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.

evidence.claim: RF emissions compliance is CISPR 11 Group 1 and Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: RF Emissions CISPR 11 Group 1 RF Emissions CISPR 11 Class A

evidence.claim: Harmonics emissions compliance is IEC 61000-3-2 Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: Harmonics Emissions IEC 61000-3-2 Class A

evidence.claim: Voltage fluctuations and flicker emissions compliance is IEC 61000-3-3.
evidence.evidenceText: Voltage fluctuations/flicker emissions IEC 61000-3-3 Complies

evidence.claim: The revision history lists Version 0.0 dated 2024-07-22 as the initial release.
evidence.evidenceText: Version Date Descriptions 0.0 2024-07-22 Initial release

evidence.claim: European Union and EEA disposal instructions state the product is not to be disposed with household waste according to WEEE Directive (2012/19/EC) and national law.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union (and EEA*) only This symbol indicates that this product is not to be disposed with your household waste, according to the WEEE Directive (2012/19/EC) and your national law.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the manual text does not explicitly identify MFDS or a Korean regulatory approval authority.
Product labels appear as section headings, but label image contents are not captured in the provided text.
Some extracted OCR text contains likely recognition or source errors, including 'disease of injury', 'Stating up', '820.11ac', and 'MBP-3S1P33330-HAZ' in the accessories list.
